---
title: "gls_fit"
generated: true
description: "Voxelwise generalised least squares with a structured residual."
---

[‚Äπ back to `nitrix.stats.lme`](../nitrix-stats-lme.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats.lme._corrfit`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gls_fit -->
```python
gls_fit(Y: Float[Array, 'V N'], X: Float[Array, 'N p'], *, group: Int[Array, 'N'], corr: Union[str, CorrSpec] = 'iid', time: Optional[Float[Array, 'N']] = None, weights: Optional[VarFunc] = None, n_iter: int = 15, damping: float = 1e-06, block: Optional[int] = None) -> GLSResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gls_fit -->
Voxelwise generalised least squares with a structured residual.

Fits, per voxel, $y_v = X \beta_v + \varepsilon_v$ with a
within-group structured residual
$\operatorname{Cov}(\varepsilon_v) = \sigma_e^2
\operatorname{diag}(g) R(\rho) \operatorname{diag}(g)$ -- `nlme`'s
`gls(correlation=corAR1 / corCAR1 / corCompSymm, weights=varPower /
varIdent)`.  $R$ is the correlation structure; $g$ the optional
variance function (heteroscedasticity).  There is no random effect here; for
a random effect *plus* a structured residual, use `fit_corr_lme`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gls_fit -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `Y` | `Float` | `V` √ó `N` | ‚Äî | `(V, N)` responses and `(N, p)` shared fixed-effect design (carries its own intercept). |
| `X` | `Float` | `N` √ó `p` | ‚Äî | `(V, N)` responses and `(N, p)` shared fixed-effect design (carries its own intercept). |
| `group` | `Int` | `N` | ‚Äî | `(N,)` integer grouping factor. The residual is correlated *within* groups and independent across them (block-diagonal `V`). |
| `corr` | `Union[str, CorrSpec]` | ‚Äî | `'iid'` | Correlation structure: `'ar1'` (discrete AR(1)), `'car1'` (continuous-time AR(1); pass `time`), `'cs'` (compound symmetry), `'iid'` (no correlation -- the default, for a pure variance-function fit), or a [`CorrSpec`](../nitrix-stats-lme/CorrSpec.qmd). |
| `time` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N']]` | ‚Äî | `None` | `(N,)` observation times for `'car1'` (and to order `'ar1'` when the rows are not already in within-group time order). Defaults to the within-group appearance order (unit gaps). |
| `weights` | `Optional[VarFunc]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Optional residual **variance function** (heteroscedasticity): [`var_power`](../nitrix-stats-lme/var_power.qmd) ($\operatorname{Var} \sim \|v\|^{2\delta}$) or [`var_ident`](../nitrix-stats-lme/var_ident.qmd) (a separate variance per stratum). Composes with any `corr`; with `corr='iid'` it is a pure heteroscedastic GLS. |
| `n_iter` | `int` | ‚Äî | `15` | Newton iterations, damping factor, and the optional voxel-block size. |
| `damping` | `float` | ‚Äî | `1e-06` | Newton iterations, damping factor, and the optional voxel-block size. |
| `block` | `Optional[int]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Newton iterations, damping factor, and the optional voxel-block size. |

**Returns** `GLSResult`. The per-voxel fit: `beta_hat`, `sigma_e_sq`, `rho`, `var_params`, `log_lik`, the GLS `fixed_cov`, and `df_resid = N - p`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:gls_fit -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map ‚Äî voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `N` | observations ‚Äî time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
